name: Chicken Noodle Soup
tagline: Bone-broth chicken soup with egg noodles, carrot, celery, dill
highlight: From-scratch broth, tender chicken, no nuts ever

hero.alt: Bowl of chicken noodle soup with carrots and dill
hero.caption: chicken noodle — broth and dill

healthFacts: Chicken bones release glycine and gelatin during simmering — gut-friendly.
Carrots provide beta-carotene; combining with the soup's fat aids absorption.

steps.title: Build the broth
steps.detail: Place chicken, halved onion, carrot ends, celery tops, bay, and 8 cups of cold water in a pot. Bring up gently to a bare simmer. Skim the gray foam.
steps.tip: Cold-water start releases more flavor than dropping into hot water.

steps.title: Simmer low
steps.detail: Cover and simmer 35 minutes — tiny lazy bubbles, never a boil. Lift chicken out and let cool. Strain broth.
steps.tip: Boiling makes cloudy broth. Patience equals clarity.

steps.title: Build soup base
steps.detail: Return broth to clean pot. Add diced carrot, celery, garlic. Simmer 10 minutes until veg are tender. Salt to taste.
steps.tip: Now is when you actually salt — concentrating an under-seasoned broth fixes it; over-salting can't be undone.

steps.title: Noodles, chicken, finish
steps.detail: Add noodles and cook to package time. Shred chicken meat off the bones and stir back in to warm through. Off heat, dill and a squeeze of lemon.
steps.tip: Cooking noodles separately if you'll have leftovers — they bloat overnight.

media.alt: Bowl of chicken noodle soup with dill
media.caption: noodles, chicken, finish
